label: Ash Wednesday
notes: Beginning of Lent. Some volunteers may attend services or observe fasting.

label: Palm Sunday
notes: Start of Holy Week. Church attendance typically high.

label: Purim
notes: Begins evening of March 2. Celebration of the saving of the Jewish people as told in the Book of Esther.

label: Passover (Pesach)
notes: Begins evening of April 1. Major holiday celebrating the Exodus from Egypt. First and last two days are most observed.

label: Good Friday
notes: Many workplaces and schools may close. Expect reduced volunteer availability.

label: Easter Sunday
notes: Major Christian holiday. Expect very low volunteer availability.

label: Shavuot
notes: Begins evening of May 21. Celebrates the giving of the Torah at Mount Sinai.

label: Rosh Hashanah
notes: Begins evening of September 11. Jewish New Year and start of the High Holy Days. Expect reduced availability for Jewish volunteers.

label: Yom Kippur
notes: Begins evening of September 20. Holiest day in Judaism; day of fasting and atonement. Expect reduced availability.

label: Sukkot
notes: Begins evening of September 25. Week-long harvest festival.

label: Christmas Eve
notes: Many volunteers will be unavailable due to family gatherings and church services.

label: Christmas Day
notes: Federal holiday. Most volunteers and organizations will be closed.

label: Hanukkah
notes: Begins evening of December 4. Eight-day Festival of Lights.

label: Christmas Day

label: Hanukkah
notes: Begins evening of December 24. Eight-day Festival of Lights. Overlaps with Christmas in 2027.
